title: Pre-monsoon alert: Rain, thunderstorms, duststorms to hit Delhi-NCR, Rajasthan, UP, Kerala and Karnataka on…; Heatwave relief for 10 days - news24online.com
full_text:
Pre-monsoon alert: Rain, thunderstorms, duststorms to hit Delhi-NCR, Rajasthan, UP, Kerala and Karnataka on…; Heatwave relief for 10 days
Pre-monsoon activity is strengthening across India, bringing rain, thunderstorms and cooler temperatures. North India is likely to remain free from heatwave conditions for the next 10 days, with widespread rainfall expected.
Pre-monsoon weather activity has become stronger across several parts of India. On 3 June, scattered to widespread rain and thunderstorms were reported in the North-East and southern peninsular regions. These weather systems helped keep daytime temperatures under control in most areas. Although temperatures crossed 40°C in a few places, no heatwave conditions were reported anywhere in the country. The highest temperature was recorded in Khammam, Telangana, at 43°C, which was 2.8°C above normal. However, it did not meet the criteria for a heatwave.
North India Escapes Severe Heat
The impact of extreme heat remained limited across the northern plains. Only a few locations recorded temperatures of 40°C or slightly above. In Rajasthan's border districts, including Jaisalmer, Phalodi, Bikaner and Sri Ganganagar, temperatures ranged between 40°C and 41°C. Even these readings were below the seasonal average. Most parts of Punjab, Haryana, Delhi and western Uttar Pradesh recorded temperatures in the mid to high 30s. At present, there are no signs of heatwave conditions in these regions.
Western Disturbance Brings Major Weather Change
A strong western disturbance over the Himalayan region, along with a cyclonic circulation over Rajasthan, is expected to keep weather conditions unstable across North India throughout the week. These systems may trigger dust storms, thunderstorms, rain and strong winds. According to weather experts, these activities are likely to be most active during the afternoon and evening hours. As a result, maximum temperatures are expected to remain close to 30°C in many places, although a few areas could still touch 40°C.
Heatwave Risk Remains Low
According to Skymet, the likelihood of a heatwave across North India will remain very low for the next 10 days. Rainfall activity is also expected to continue in several states.
Areas Likely to Receive Rain
Light to moderate rain, with isolated heavy showers, is expected over Kerala, Lakshadweep and the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. Scattered light to moderate rainfall is also likely in:
Parts of Rajasthan may witness dust storms and thunderstorms.
Meanwhile, isolated light rain and thunderstorm activity is possible in Delhi, southern Odisha, coastal Andhra Pradesh and Gujarat.
